page.title: Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]
{{Japanese law | title = Establishing Holidays for Annual Festivals and National Holidays | status = Abolished | type = {{ill|Administrative Procedure Act (Japan)|en|Administrative Procedure Act (Japan)|qid=Q4683484|lt=Administrative Procedure Law|1=Administrative Procedure Act (Japan)}} | content = | related = | link = [{{NDLDC|787953/335}} 法令全書 明治6th year ] | wikisource = 年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム }} '''Establishing Holidays for Annual Festivals and National Holidays''' (年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム, Meiji 6, October 14, Daijōkan Proclamation No. 344) was a {{ill|Daijō-kan's proclamation|ja|太政官布告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan Proclamation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官布告・太政官達|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11444008|lt=Daijō-kan proclamation|1=Dajōkan Edict}} of Japan concerning {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}). == Overview == It was promulgated and came into effect on {{ill|October 14, 1873|ja|1873rd year 10月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 14, 1873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a {{ill|Daijō-kan's proclamation|ja|太政官布告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan Proclamation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官布告・太政官達|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11444008|lt=Daijō-kan proclamation|1=Dajōkan Edict}}. Initially, the holidays were {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ({{ill|January 3|en|January 3|qid=Q2156|lt=January 3|1=January 3}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11502138}} ({{ill|January 5|en|January 5|qid=Q2202|lt=January 5|1=January 5}}), {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}}; {{ill|January 30|en|January 30|qid=Q2287|lt=January 30|1=January 30}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|qid=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} ({{ill|September 17|en|September 17|qid=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}}), {{ill|The Emperor's Birthday|ja|天長節|The Emperor's Birthday=The Emperor's Birthday|lt=Tenchōsetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Emperor's Birthday|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|qid=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}}), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}), totaling 8 days. In {{ill|1878|en|1878|qid=Q7776|lt=1878|1=1878}} (Meiji 11, June 5), an amendment added the {{ill|Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|qid=Q1339268|lt=Spring Equinox|1=Chunfen}}) and {{ill|Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|qid=Q1200436|lt=Autumn Equinox|1=Qiufen}}), increasing the holidays to 10 days. In {{ill|1879|en|1879|qid=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12, July 5), another amendment changed the date of {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} from {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|qid=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}} to {{ill|October 17|en|October 17|qid=Q2962|lt=October 17|1=October 17}}. This adjustment was made because {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|qid=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}} in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=New Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} occurred too early in the agricultural season for rice crops to fully mature, necessitating a {{ill|month delay|ja|月遅れ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/month delay|1=month delay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516230}}. On {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1), it was abolished with the promulgation and implementation of the {{ill|Regarding Holidays|ja|休日ニ関スル件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regarding Holidays|qq=}}.
== References == * "Meiji Period Law Compendium, Volume 6-2" (1975, Hara Shobo) == Related Articles == {{Wikisource|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} * {{ill|non-working day|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-working day|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}} * {{ill|Imperial Household Religious Service Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Religious Service Ordinance|1=Imperial Household Rites Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581038}} * {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421011}}
{{translated page|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|version=99056790|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]
